name: Kigelia Sausage Tree Extract
description: A revered indigenous tree producing large sausage-shaped fruits. Rich in naphthoquinones (pinnatal) and iridoids providing verified anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and tissue-reconstructive capabilities for tropical ulcers and chronic skin sores.
preparation: Topical paste: Crush dry mature fruit/bark into fine powder, blend with clean warm water or pure petroleum jelly, and apply directly onto tropical ulcers or fungal lesions twice daily.
safetyAlert: Unripe raw fruit is highly purgative and emetic. Only harvest mature dry pods or bark for preparations.

demographicCautions.title: Pediatric Precaution
demographicCautions.message: Only apply topically in low concentrations. Avoid any oral ingestion of kigelia extracts in children under 5.

sideEffects: Mild localized contact dermatitis or skin warmth upon initial topical application.
contraindications: Open systemic septic wounds, active deep bleeding sores, or hypersensitivity.
countyStorageAdvisory: Mombasa: Store ground dried fruit powder in dry airtight metal canisters to block seaside saline moisture. Lodwar: Keep whole dried fruit slices in a dry, ventilated hessian sack hanging off the ground to prevent insect rot.
dosingRules: Children under 5: Topical only, diluted to a max 5% concentration in a carrier oil or petroleum jelly. Adults: Apply 10% to 20% concentrated paste topically to the margins of clean debrided sores twice daily.
